front: What is the primary function of a decoder in digital circuits?
back: A decoder is a combinational circuit that converts binary information from n input lines to a maximum of 2^n unique output lines.

front: How many inputs does a 3-to-8 line decoder have, and how many outputs?
back: A 3-to-8 line decoder has 3 inputs and 8 outputs (2^3 = 8).

front: What is the purpose of an enable input on a decoder?
back: The enable input controls the operation of the decoder. When disabled, all outputs are inactive (usually 0), regardless of the input code.

front: How can two 3-to-8 line decoders be combined to create a 4-to-16 line decoder?
back: The fourth input line is used as an enable signal. One decoder is enabled when the 4th input is 0, the other when it is 1, effectively doubling the output combinations to 16.

front: Besides code conversion, name two common applications of decoders.
back: 1) Address decoding in memory systems. 2) Implementing simple Boolean functions (acting as a minterm generator).
